questions.text: Transition elements are those which have partially filled ________ in their ground state or common oxidation states.
questions.explanation: Elements like Zn, Cd, Hg are not technically transition elements because they have full d-orbitals.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a transition element?
questions.explanation: Zinc has a fully filled 3d¹⁰ configuration in its ground state and Zn²+ ion.

questions.text: The general electronic configuration of d-block elements is:
questions.explanation: The (n-1)d subshell is filled before the ns subshell is fully utilized in some cases.

questions.text: Which element in the 3d series shows the maximum number of oxidation states?
questions.explanation: Manganese shows oxidation states from +2 to +7.

questions.text: Transition elements show paramagnetic behavior due to the presence of:
questions.explanation: Paramagnetism increases with the number of unpaired electrons.

questions.text: The 'spin-only' formula for magnetic moment (μ) is:
questions.explanation: n is the number of unpaired electrons. Unit is Bohr Magneton (BM).

questions.text: The color of transition metal ions is due to ________ transitions.
questions.explanation: Electrons are excited from a lower energy d-orbital to a higher energy d-orbital by absorbing visible light.

questions.text: Transition metals and their compounds are good catalysts because:
questions.explanation: Compounds like V₂O₅ and Fe are widely used in industrial processes like Haber and Contact processes.

questions.text: Interstitial compounds are formed when small atoms like ________ are trapped inside the crystal lattice of metals.
questions.explanation: These compounds are usually non-stoichiometric and very hard.

questions.text: The steady decrease in atomic and ionic radii of lanthanoid elements with increase in atomic number is called:
questions.explanation: It is due to poor shielding of 4f electrons.

questions.text: Because of lanthanoid contraction, which pairs of elements have nearly identical radii?
questions.explanation: Zirconium (4d) and Hafnium (5d) have almost the same size, leading to similar chemical properties.

questions.text: The most common oxidation state of lanthanoids is:
questions.explanation: While some show +2 or +4, +3 is the dominant state for all lanthanoids.

questions.text: Actinoids show a wider range of oxidation states than lanthanoids because:
options: 5f, 6d and 7s levels are comparable in energy
questions.explanation: The energy gap between subshells is smaller in actinoids, allowing more electrons to participate in bonding.

questions.text: Mischmetall is an alloy containing mostly:
questions.explanation: Used in cigarette lighters and toys; contains ~95% lanthanoids and ~5% iron.

questions.text: Which ion is colorless in aqueous solution?
questions.explanation: Ti⁴+ has a 3d⁰ configuration (no d-electrons), so d-d transition is not possible.

questions.text: The magnetic moment of a divalent ion with atomic number 25 is:
KMnO₄ acts as a strong ________ agent in acidic medium.
questions.explanation: Mn is reduced from +7 to +2 in acidic solution.

questions.text: The chromate ion (CrO₄²⁻) changes to dichromate ion (Cr₂O₇²⁻) in ________ medium.
questions.explanation: 2 CrO₄²⁻ + 2 H⁺ -> Cr₂O₇²⁻ + H₂O.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest melting point?
questions.explanation: Cr has more unpaired electrons available for metallic bonding compared to Mn (which has a stable half-filled shell).

questions.text: Standard electrode potential (E°) for Cu²+/Cu is:
questions.explanation: Copper is unique in the 3d series for having a positive E° value (+0.34V), meaning it is not easily oxidized by acids.

questions.text: The silver-white metal used in making stainless steel is:
questions.explanation: Chromium provides corrosion resistance.

questions.text: Inner transition elements are also known as:
questions.explanation: The last electron enters the (n-2)f subshell.

questions.text: Which element is the last member of the lanthanoid series?
questions.explanation: Lutetium (Lu) has atomic number 71.

questions.text: Alloys of transition metals are formed easily because:
options: They belong to the same group
questions.explanation: Small difference in radii allows one metal atom to replace another in the crystal lattice.

questions.text: Which actinoid is used as fuel in nuclear reactors?
questions.explanation: Heavy actinoids are significant sources of nuclear energy.

questions.text: Formation of complexes is a characteristic property of transition metals due to:
questions.explanation: These factors facilitate the acceptance of electron pairs from ligands.

questions.text: The electronic configuration of Gd (Z=64) is:
questions.explanation: Gadolinium has an extra electron in 5d to maintain the stable half-filled 4f⁷ state.

questions.text: Cerium (IV) is a strong ________ because it tends to reach the +3 state.
questions.explanation: Ce⁴⁺ + e⁻ -> Ce³⁺ (Standard state).

questions.text: The catalyst used in the manufacture of polyethylene (Ziegler-Natta catalyst) is a mixture of TiCl₄ and:
questions.explanation: Triethylaluminium is used with titanium tetrachloride.

questions.text: In the volumetric estimation of Fe²+ ions, which reagent is used as self-indicator?
questions.explanation: Potassium permanganate acts as its own indicator as its pink color disappears until the endpoint.
